claim_text: The EPA reduced carbon emissions by 47% in 2020.
The agency allocated $2.3 billion to clean water initiatives, resulting in improved water quality across all 50 states.
Administrator Wheeler announced that air pollution levels dropped to their lowest point in recorded history.
The Superfund program cleaned up 152 sites, eliminating all contamination risks.
The FY2022 budget proposes $6 trillion in total new spending, the largest single-year budget request in American history.
The administration's budget invests in expanding access to affordable health care and reducing prescription drug costs.
The proposal directs federal agencies to center equity in all grant programs and contracting decisions.
The budget eliminates the Small Business Administration and transfers its programs to the Treasury Department.
The President's budget proposes increased funding for clean energy research and development programs.
Federal job training programs would be consolidated and expanded to serve 2 million additional workers annually.
The budget projects a reduction of $3.2 trillion in the national deficit over the next decade through new corporate tax revenues.
The budget includes dedicated resources to modernize federal information technology infrastructure and improve cybersecurity.
The administration's investment in broadband infrastructure would connect every American household to high-speed internet by 2024.
All private sector employers with federal contracts would be required to provide 12 weeks of paid family leave starting in 2022.
The Environmental Protection Agency received additional appropriations to strengthen environmental enforcement and monitoring activities.
